The Agentic App Ecosystem

1,551 apps across three AI platforms, split by interaction model. Interface agents (ChatGPT, Claude) act on behalf of users through a chat UI. Code agents (Gemini CLI) are developer extensions run from the terminal.

Interface Agents
User chats → AI acts on their behalf via connected apps
ChatGPT Claude
Code Agents
Developer runs CLI → extensions execute in the terminal
Gemini CLI
1,551
Agentic apps across 3 AI platforms
ChatGPT (375) · Claude (118) · Gemini CLI (1,058)
59%
Software & Development tools
913 of 1,551 apps are developer-oriented — the agentic ecosystem is still overwhelmingly built by and for developers
8
Cross-platform apps
Only 8 apps bridge both paradigms — all technical or business tools. Zero consumer apps cross the divide
3
Underserved domains
News (6 apps), Health & Fitness (5), Education (31) — the agentic gap in everyday life

Key Findings

The ecosystem is structurally bifurcated. Interface agents (ChatGPT, Claude) and code agents (Gemini CLI) serve fundamentally different users with almost no overlap. They represent two parallel visions of agentic AI: consumer delegation vs developer automation.
ChatGPT is the only truly consumer-diverse platform. With 26% Management, 21% Lifestyle, 12% Productivity, and 7% Creativity, it's the only platform seriously building the "do things for me" vision across everyday domains.
Claude sits between both worlds. Despite being an interface agent, 78% of Claude's apps are AI & Programming tools (MCP servers). Its ecosystem profile is structurally closer to Gemini's code agents than to ChatGPT's consumer connectors. Claude is a developer's interface agent.
Gemini CLI dominates in volume but not in breadth. With 1,058 extensions — 94% in AI & Programming — it reflects the open-source developer community building tooling around a new CLI paradigm, but hasn't expanded beyond its core technical audience.
The agentic gap is wide. Categories like News, Health & Fitness, and Education have minimal representation. The promise of AI agents managing everyday life tasks is largely unfulfilled outside of travel, shopping, and business tools.
Janna Joceli Omena & Claude
Data curation & merging by Alice Figueiredo & Janna Joceli Omena
